frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Mohammed: Aaj kal ki ladkiyan bhi na, office jaane ke naam pe itna makeup kar leti hain jaise asli chehra chhupana ho. Thoda sa decent rehna theek hai, lekin zyada ho toh bas attention ke liye lagta hai; aur bilkul tayyar na ho toh phir bolte hain apna khayal nahi rakhti. Natural rehna hi behtar hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: condemning 'too much' makeup as deceptive/attention-seeking while also criticizing women who do not maintain appearance. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and appearance obligation central to the makeup paradox.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
